title: life after 3I or the lack thereof
display_title: life after 3I or the lack thereof
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Aug 14, 2013 7:19 pm Chuckman wrote: I'd have to set it in a timeline with no 3I, obviously.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Aug 14, 2013 7:21 pm In my mind, Post-3I and any kind of normalcy don't mix, at least not within any reasonable time frame.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Aug 14, 2013 7:28 pm Chuckman wrote: In my mind, Post-3I and any kind of normalcy don't mix, at least not within any reasonable time frame.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Wed Aug 14, 2013 7:57 pm Bagheera wrote: Ten years is pretty reasonable, IMO. Don't forget that in the canon setting people recovered from literally losing half the world's population in a mere 15 years. The precedent alone is cause for SoD.

[]:             Quote Post by gorgeousshutin » Wed Aug 14, 2013 8:05 pm Well, if EoE says people can "imagine themselves in their hearts" and come back with personal belongings - thus making clothes, accessories, I.D., Visa Cards + other crucial personal items all handy - then I don't see how the post 3I chaos could last for too long . . .

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Aug 14, 2013 8:07 pm Lavinius wrote: Except Second Impact was on an entirely different scale. A few cities sunk, a few people died. Blah. They could even call it a natural disaster, and we'd believe them.

[]:             Quote Post by Muphrid » Wed Aug 14, 2013 8:47 pm Bagheera wrote: Er, yeah. Different, as in larger . A continent blew up, dude. You don't even want to think about the effect that would have on the planet. 3I was nothing by comparison. Yes, it was disruptive since people got moved around and all, but the actual effect on the planet was trivial. Between the remaining S2 engines and the post-2I Nerv tech restoring the world won't be that big of a deal. Bad for a few years, yes, but utterly resolved ten years down the line. Dragging it out for much longer would be the bigger violation of SoD.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Thu Aug 15, 2013 12:03 am That's something a lot of post-apocalypse stories fail to take into account: without people to maintain them, structures, material, everything begins to degrade and erode. Even with people appearing fairly rapidly, you would still have large segments of infrastructure that would begin to break down and collapse. That's not taking into account who is coming back (what were they doing before Impact? Were they engineers, doctors, municipal workers?) That's less of an issue of course... Then again, there are those that were probably tanged while driving vehicles...enough of those vehicles crash in awkward areas, and you're back to the issue of infrastructure damage.

[]:             Quote Post by Muphrid » Thu Aug 15, 2013 12:57 am Quite right. I think the problem is exacerbated if people don't all come back or don't all come back at once. If it's a gradual thing, then not all that infrastructure can be maintained. Some areas can't be serviced and fall into disrepair and disuse, opening those areas up to looting.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 15, 2013 12:59 am Gob Hobblin wrote: That's something a lot of post-apocalypse stories fail to take into account: without people to maintain them, structures, material, everything begins to degrade and erode. Even with people appearing fairly rapidly, you would still have large segments of infrastructure that would begin to break down and collapse. That's not taking into account who is coming back (what were they doing before Impact? Were they engineers, doctors, municipal workers?) That's less of an issue of course... Then again, there are those that were probably tanged while driving vehicles...enough of those vehicles crash in awkward areas, and you're back to the issue of infrastructure damage.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Aug 15, 2013 1:49 am I don't think it's unreasonable to assume that s2 generators and weapons that are based on angels were also on the 'turned to stone' list.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 15, 2013 2:10 am NemZ wrote: I don't think it's unreasonable to assume that s2 generators and weapons that are based on angels were also on the 'turned to stone' list.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Thu Aug 15, 2013 4:02 am Because only a handful of people know how to use them.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Aug 15, 2013 4:13 am Do we even know that S2 generators exist outside of evas? S2 weapons, yes, but power plants? If they did episode 6's power needs make no sense at all. Considering a magi unit runs on cloned brain cells it's entirely possible they were tanged along with all other organic matter on the planet.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 15, 2013 5:57 am NemZ wrote: Do we even know that S2 generators exist outside of evas? S2 weapons, yes, but power plants? If they did episode 6's power needs make no sense at all.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Aug 15, 2013 6:28 am NemZ wrote: Do we even know that S2 generators exist outside of evas? S2 weapons, yes, but power plants? If they did episode 6's power needs make no sense at all.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Aug 15, 2013 6:31 am Bagheera wrote: I've never heard of S2 weapons.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 15, 2013 7:44 am NemZ wrote: My point is there's a lot more assumption involved in all these eva-based technologies you think could help bootstrap everything back into shape, and even before 3I the world was on the verge of economic and social collapse. Pulling that off within the next few generations would still require a lot of lucky breaks.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Thu Aug 15, 2013 10:32 am Well, that's also assuming EVA tech survived the Third Impact. If all of Evangelion devices were derived from Adam (with the exception of Unit-01), where would those devices fit into a post-3I world? Would there still be the issue of Adam vs. Lilith? It's not saying Evangelion tech couldn't survive, seeing as there's no indications one way or the other. It is something interesting to think about, though. As for the MAGI, it is something overlooked (I overlooked it, myself). Then again, one has to take in mind the design behind MAGI: is it extremely durable, or does it require constant maintenance. When I did my first 3I story, I ignored it on the basis that there were simply not enough people who came back in time with the knowledge of how to use MAGI, and that it and other portions of the GeoFront (that hadn't been flooded or crushed) were salvaged for other purposes. Bear in mind that portions of MAGI (significant portions) are protein based, and while this might make it a more efficient supercomputer, it also makes it more vulnerable to rot and core-system failure via hardware degradation (unless it's some sort of super material, which it COULD be...). Still, the presence of a MAGI or multiple MAGI systems could lead to interesting post 3I scenarios, especially if they had automated systems within the bases to maintain their own internal infrastructure. You could see a social rift from between those with physical access to the bases (former personnel on varying security rosters) versus the greater masses who do not. I think I gave myself a new story idea...

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 15, 2013 10:51 am Gob Hobblin wrote: Well, that's also assuming EVA tech survived the Third Impact. If all of Evangelion devices were derived from Adam (with the exception of Unit-01), where would those devices fit into a post-3I world? Would there still be the issue of Adam vs. Lilith? It's not saying Evangelion tech couldn't survive, seeing as there's no indications one way or the other. It is something interesting to think about, though.
